title: Post-operative respiratory concern documentation

red_flags.text: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.
red_flags.rationale: The exact ASA recovery section supports clinician-documented respiratory and cardiovascular status and symptom review after anesthesia; it does not infer a complication or direct treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.

exact_section.heading: Patient assessment and monitoring during recovery

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: This section was reviewed for OSA-related postoperative risk but was not mapped because the legacy workflow does not establish OSA or the relevant procedure and analgesic context.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea

medication.text: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.
medication.rationale: This section was reviewed for oxygenation, ventilation, and consciousness documentation but was not mapped because it applies only when neuraxial opioid exposure is established.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection, and Management of Respiratory Depression Associated with Neuraxial Opioid Administration

follow_up.text: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.
follow_up.rationale: The current ASA standard supports recording an already completed transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover for a postoperative respiratory concern.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.

exact_section.heading: Standards I–II — PACU transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover

sources_used.title: Practice Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection, and Management of Respiratory Depression Associated with Neuraxial Opioid Administration
Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea

source_limitations: Procedure and anesthetic technique, airway events, extubation circumstances, opioid or sedative exposure, oxygen support, respiratory rate, work of breathing, stridor, wheeze, aspiration concern, altered consciousness, hemodynamics, baseline lung disease, examination detail, investigation status, escalation, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
